moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% for more income and broad index stability.
Establish a 15% cash buffer for capital preservation.
This plan prioritizes safety and lower drawdown over aggressive growth.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets around 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower volatility. Expect modest returns with reduced downside risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Sell SOXL to exit a position below its stop and reduce overall leverage.
Trim QQQI and SMH to reduce exposure to downtrending assets.
Buy ANET and PANW to add high-quality tech with strong scanner reads.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer for flexibility and risk management.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets around 2.5% per month, prioritizing risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and diversifies into strong scanner setups. Expect moderate returns with controlled drawdown.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with concentration limits.
Sell SOXL to exit a position below its stop, freeing capital.
Trim QQQI and SMH to reduce exposure to downtrends.
Buy ANET and NVDU, concentrating in high-scoring tech setups.
Add TECS for leveraged inverse tech exposure, managing overall sector risk.
Maintain a small 5% cash buffer, leaning into market exposure.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets around 4% per month, stretching for growth. It leans into leverage and concentrates in high-scoring setups, which amplifies both upside and drawdown. Be aware of daily decay in leveraged ETFs and the potential for significant losses if stops are hit.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 15% to reduce concentration and shift focus.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Sell CRWD and SMH to exit high-beta single names and sector ETFs.
Increase QQQI to 40% for maximum income-focused exposure.
Establish a 20% cash buffer to prioritize low drawdown and stability.
income.read: This income approach targets around 1% per month, prioritizing steady distributions and capital preservation. It significantly reduces high-beta exposure and leverage. While aiming for reliable income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market downturns.
